commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cd2421a0fd2d4ba9003765d554e2aa7aa1531de0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram dispatch to suppress text-only post-final tool/error payloads and adds a regression test for late failed tool/final payloads after a final reply.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +20. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: core final payload delivery loops over all replies, while core payload construction deliberately emits an assistant success reply followed by an `isError` mutating-tool warning. The PR-head Telegram guard drops that second final once the first answer starts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A code repair is needed, but the external PR also lacks required real Telegram proof, so this should stay human-gated rather than requesting automated repair markers.

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/error payloads once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve pre-final tool failures, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Add a regression test for late failed tool/final payloads after a final reply
- Add inline rationale for why post-final handled tool errors are intentionally dropped

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

This keeps actionable failures visible before the final answer, while suppressing late text-only failure notifications that the LLM already absorbed.

## Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
- git diff --check
